behavior_summary: U8P_FIRE::func0080 runs as a timed process bound to a FIRE item instance and advances the item's animation/frame over time (read via Item::getFrame and written via Item::setFrame).

At specific animation frames (notably frame == 0x02 and other hex thresholds like 0x0F, 0x1D, 0x23, 0x2C) it computes nearby coordinates from Item::getX/getY/getZ and attempts to create effect items using Item::legal_create. On successful create it spawns behaviors on the new item (LAVA::func00AF or PYROS::func20A1(0x01)).

The routine uses a spawned helper (FREE::func1C19) and synchronizes/waits on it (spawn/compare to local2 and suspend) to pace or coordinate operations, and it calls FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) and PYROS::func3E82 when activation/resetting is required.

Internal boolean-like locals (local5, local4) act as guards so spawns and reset/notification happen only once per activation window; the process loops with suspend delays until lifecycle completion/reset.
notable_control_flow: Main loop is time-driven: read frame -> conditional branches for frame thresholds -> perform spawn/create attempts or toggles -> Item::setFrame -> suspend (delay) -> repeat.
Frame-specific branches: a creation/activation block at frame == 0x02, additional behavior changes at frames 0x0F, 0x1D, 0x23, and 0x2C forming phased lifecycle transitions.
Spawning and synchronization: the process may spawn FREE::func1C19 and compare its handle with local2 to wait for or detect completion before continuing.
Guard logic: local5 and local4 are toggled and checked to prevent repeated resets/spawns and to gate calls to FREE::resetRef and PYROS::func3E82.
open_questions: What exact gameplay semantics correspond to each frame threshold (0x02, 0x0F, 0x1D, 0x23, 0x2C)? — these appear to be animation/lifecycle phases but mapping to visible game events is inferred.
What is the precise effect and contract of FREE::func1C19 (spawned helper) and how long/what conditions does it enforce before the process continues?
What does the registry key 0x0202 (used with FREE::resetRef) represent in game data and how does resetRef affect other systems?
Is local4 used both as a created-item reference and later as a boolean-like flag (inference from mixed use); this dual role needs verification from original source or runtime traces.

main_function.name: U8P_FIRE::func0080
main_function.purpose: Drive the FIRE item instance's animation and side-effects over time: advance frames, attempt to spawn nearby flame/lava effects at specific frames, coordinate timing with an external helper, and perform cleanup/notification when activation occurs.
entry_conditions: Spawned/started as a process attached to a FIRE item instance (invoked externally, e.g., from PYROS::func247C per available metadata).
Runs continuously for the lifetime of the FIRE item, paced by suspend/delay operations.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRE::func0080
function_map.role: Manage FIRE item lifecycle, animation progression, spawn nearby effects, and perform cleanup/notification.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Read the item's current animation/frame index (used to drive conditional behavior).

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Write a new frame value to progress the item's animation/lifecycle.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Provide item's X coordinate for computing spawn offsets.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Provide item's Y coordinate for computing spawn offsets.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Provide item's Z/level for spawn placement computations.

function_map.name: Item::legal_create
function_map.role: Check/attempt creation of a new item/entity at a candidate position; returns created-item reference via out parameter when allowed.

function_map.name: LAVA::func00AF
function_map.role: Behavior spawned on newly created lava-effect items (initialization of LAVA behavior).

function_map.name: PYROS::func20A1
function_map.role: Behavior spawned on created pyro items (called with flag 0x01); initializes alternate fire effect.

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper/process used for synchronization/timing with the main loop (exact purpose inferred).

function_map.name: FREE::resetRef
function_map.role: Deregister/reset a registry/reference (called with 0x0202 and this) as part of activation/cleanup.

function_map.name: PYROS::func3E82
function_map.role: Notify or trigger a PYROS-system update after reset/activation (inferred side-effect).

external_calls.purpose: Read current animation/frame index to decide behavior and when to advance frames.
Obtain item's X coordinate to compute spawn X offsets for creation attempts.
Obtain item's Y coordinate to compute spawn Y offsets for creation attempts.
Obtain item's Z coordinate/level to compute spawn Z for creation attempts.
Attempt to create nearby effect items/entities at computed offsets; returns created reference via out parameter.
Initialize LAVA behavior on a successfully created lava item (spawned coroutine).
Initialize PYROS/pyro behavior on a created item (alternate creation path), called with flag 0x01.
Spawn a helper process used for timing/synchronization; return/handle is compared with local2 and used to suspend/wait.
Reset/deregister a reference (0x0202, this) as part of activation/cleanup before notifying PYROS.
Notify the PYROS system of activation/reset after FREE::resetRef is called.
Write back the incremented/updated frame to progress the item's animation.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_FIRE::func0080 runs as a timed process bound to a FIRE item instance and advances the item's animation/frame over time (read via Item::getFrame and written via Item::setFrame).
